mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides an integrated Docker experience with Kubernetes support for local development. It simplifies the setup of a Kubernetes cluster on macOS, allowing developers to easily test and deploy applications. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to cloud solutions and reliance on macOS-specific features.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the Docker Desktop download page.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

sudo cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil unmount /Volumes/Docker

```

> Replace 'Docker.dmg' with the actual downloaded file name if different.
> This copies Docker Desktop to your Applications folder.
> This unmounts the Docker installer volume.

steps.title: Start Docker Desktop and Enable Kubernetes
steps.description:
Launch Docker Desktop and enable Kubernetes support in the settings.

```bash

open -a Docker

sleep 30

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to activate'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Preferences" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Preferences" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

```

> This starts Docker Desktop.
> Wait for Docker to initialize.
> This brings Docker Desktop to the foreground.
> This opens the preferences.
> This enables Kubernetes.
> This applies the changes and restarts Docker.
commands: osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Preferences" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Preferences" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check if Kubernetes is running and accessible via kubectl.

```bash

kubectl cluster-info

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command shows the Kubernetes cluster information.
> This command lists the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This will remove Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to quit'

sudo rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

rm -rf ~/.docker

```

> This quits Docker Desktop.
> This removes Docker Desktop from the Applications folder.
> This removes Docker configuration and data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Backup your Docker data, upgrade to the latest version, and verify the installation.

```bash

docker save -o ~/docker-backup.tar docker-image-name

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

sudo cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'docker-image-name' with your actual image names.
> Download the latest version.
> Open the downloaded installer.
> Replace the old version with the new one.
> Verify that Kubernetes is still running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have started Kubernetes properly.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and ensure Kubernetes is enabled in settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl: command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure Docker Desktop is installed and kubectl is included in the installation.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server is not running.

**Fix:**
Check Docker Desktop settings to ensure Kubernetes is enabled and running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes cluster may be taking too long to start.

**Fix:**
Wait for a few minutes and check the status again with 'kubectl cluster-info'.

versionNotes.changes: Improved Kubernetes integration and added support for Kubernetes v1.30.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'Kubernetes master is running at https://127.0.0.1:6443' and a list of nodes when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker"'

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
